title: hieracium bupleuroides subsp. arcuatum
intro: Hieracium bupleuroides subsp. arcuatum is a lesser-known species of the Hieracium genus, commonly referred to as a type of hawkweed. It is a perennial plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant's leaves and flowers are used in herbal preparations, which are believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is used to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and digestive issues.
